Title: CsYABBY1 and CsMYB114 Enhance Acquired Drought Tolerance by Mediating Flavonoid Biosynthesis in Camellia sinensis

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core entry step of flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA units to form naringenin chalcone, initiating flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant ROS scavenging and enhancement of drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in tea shoots; strongly upregulated in drought-primed plants under recurrent drought, especially C2-T2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonoid accumulation and enhances drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in tea plants; flavonoid profiling; PEG6000 drought assay; chlorophyll fluorescence; MDA/H2O2/O2·− measurements
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsCHS expression was significantly higher in drought-primed tea plants under recurrent drought, especially C2-T2. 2. CsCHS overexpression increased total flavonoid content. 3. CsCHS overexpression reduced oxidative damage and improved drought tolerance under PEG6000 treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: BIG Data Center PRJCA039106; tissue-expression reference SRA PRJNA665594; untargeted metabolomics accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Moderate drought priming; recurrent drought stress; 15% PEG6000 for 24 h
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct target of both CsYABBY1 and CsMYB114 by Y1H, dual-LUC and EMSA.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes chalcone to flavanone in the early flavonoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant ROS scavenging and enhancement of drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in tea shoots; strongly upregulated in drought-primed plants under recurrent drought, especially C2-T2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonoid accumulation and enhances drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in tea plants; flavonoid quantification; PEG6000 drought assay; chlorophyll fluorescence; MDA/H2O2/O2·− measurements
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsCHI was significantly induced in drought-primed tea plants during recurrent drought. 2. CsCHI overexpression elevated total flavonoids. 3. CsCHI overexpression improved physiological drought tolerance and lowered ROS-associated damage.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: BIG Data Center PRJCA039106; tissue-expression reference SRA PRJNA665594; untargeted metabolomics accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Moderate drought priming; recurrent drought stress; 15% PEG6000 for 24 h
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct target of both CsYABBY1 and CsMYB114 by Y1H, dual-LUC and EMSA.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts dihydroflavonols to flavonols in the flavonol branch of flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant ROS scavenging and enhancement of drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in tea shoots; strongly upregulated in drought-primed plants under recurrent drought, especially C2-T2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonoid accumulation and enhances drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in tea plants; flavonoid quantification; PEG6000 drought assay; chlorophyll fluorescence; MDA/H2O2/O2·− measurements
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsFLS expression increased in drought-primed tea plants under repeated drought. 2. CsFLS overexpression elevated total flavonoid content. 3. CsFLS overexpression improved drought tolerance and reduced oxidative injury.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: BIG Data Center PRJCA039106; tissue-expression reference SRA PRJNA665594; untargeted metabolomics accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Moderate drought priming; recurrent drought stress; 15% PEG6000 for 24 h
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct target of both CsYABBY1 and CsMYB114 by Y1H, dual-LUC and EMSA.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins, feeding anthocyanidin and proanthocyanidin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant ROS scavenging and enhancement of drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in tea shoots; strongly upregulated in drought-primed plants under recurrent drought, especially C2-T2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonoid accumulation and enhances drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in tea plants; flavonoid quantification; PEG6000 drought assay; chlorophyll fluorescence; MDA/H2O2/O2·− measurements
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsDFR showed significantly higher expression in drought-primed tea plants under recurrent drought. 2. CsDFR overexpression increased total flavonoids. 3. CsDFR overexpression reduced drought-induced oxidative damage and improved tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: BIG Data Center PRJCA039106; tissue-expression reference SRA PRJNA665594; untargeted metabolomics accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Moderate drought priming; recurrent drought stress; 15% PEG6000 for 24 h
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct target of CsMYB114 by Y1H, dual-LUC and EMSA; not directly bound by CsYABBY1 in Y1H.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins in the late flavonoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant ROS scavenging and enhancement of drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in tea shoots; strongly upregulated in drought-primed plants under recurrent drought, especially C2-T2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonoid accumulation and enhances drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in tea plants; flavonoid quantification; PEG6000 drought assay; chlorophyll fluorescence; MDA/H2O2/O2·− measurements
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsANS was strongly induced in drought-primed tea plants under recurrent drought. 2. CsANS overexpression elevated flavonoid accumulation. 3. CsANS overexpression alleviated drought-induced oxidative stress and improved tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: BIG Data Center PRJCA039106; tissue-expression reference SRA PRJNA665594; untargeted metabolomics accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Moderate drought priming; recurrent drought stress; 15% PEG6000 for 24 h
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct target of both CsYABBY1 and CsMYB114 by Y1H, dual-LUC and EMSA.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: flavan-3-ols/proanthocyanidin precursors such as epicatechin-type products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces anthocyanidins to flavan-3-ol products in the proanthocyanidin branch.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant ROS scavenging and enhancement of drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in tea shoots; strongly upregulated in drought-primed plants under recurrent drought, especially C2-T2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonoid accumulation and enhances drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in tea plants; flavonoid quantification; PEG6000 drought assay; chlorophyll fluorescence; MDA/H2O2/O2·− measurements
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsANR expression was elevated in drought-primed tea plants under repeated drought. 2. CsANR overexpression increased total flavonoid content. 3. CsANR overexpression enhanced drought tolerance and reduced oxidative injury.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: BIG Data Center PRJCA039106; tissue-expression reference SRA PRJNA665594; untargeted metabolomics accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Moderate drought priming; recurrent drought stress; 15% PEG6000 for 24 h
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct target of both CsYABBY1 and CsMYB114 by Y1H, dual-LUC and EMSA.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: YABBY family; N-terminal C2C2 zinc-finger domain and C-terminal YABBY domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant ROS scavenging and enhancement of drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in shoots (bud and young leaf); higher in drought-primed plants, especially under second-cycle moderate drought
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in shoots (bud and young leaf)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CsCHS, CsCHI, CsFLS, CsANS and CsANR expression; overexpression also upregulated five flavonoid biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Moderate drought priming; recurrent drought stress
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key hub activator contributing to acquired drought tolerance by increasing flavonoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances flavonoid accumulation, ROS scavenging capacity and drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA; overexpression in tea plants; AsODN-mediated silencing; Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA; molecular docking; qRT-PCR; metabolite quantification; drought phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsYABBY1 was identified as a hub TF positively correlated with six flavonoid biosynthesis genes and induced by drought priming. 2. CsYABBY1 directly bound promoters of CsCHS, CsCHI, CsFLS, CsANS and CsANR and activated their transcription. 3. Overexpression increased flavonoid content and drought tolerance, whereas silencing reduced flavonoids and impaired drought tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: BIG Data Center PRJCA039106; tissue-expression reference SRA PRJNA665594
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Moderate drought priming; recurrent drought stress; 15% PEG6000 for 24 h
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transcriptional activator in yeast and tobacco. Molecular docking supported promoter binding. DFR was not supported as a direct target in Y1H.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant ROS scavenging and enhancement of drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in shoots (bud and young leaf); higher in drought-primed plants, especially under second-cycle moderate drought
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in shoots (bud and young leaf)
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CsCHS; CsCHI; CsFLS; CsDFR; CsANS; CsANR
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CsCHS, CsCHI, CsFLS, CsDFR, CsANS and CsANR expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Moderate drought priming; recurrent drought stress
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key hub activator contributing to acquired drought tolerance by broadly activating flavonoid biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances flavonoid accumulation, ROS scavenging capacity and drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA; overexpression in tea plants; AsODN-mediated silencing; Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA; molecular docking; qRT-PCR; metabolite quantification; drought phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMYB114 was identified as a hub TF induced by drought priming and positively correlated with flavonoid pathway genes. 2. CsMYB114 directly bound promoters of CsCHS, CsCHI, CsFLS, CsDFR, CsANS and CsANR and activated their transcription. 3. Overexpression increased flavonoids and drought tolerance, whereas silencing decreased pathway gene expression, flavonoid content and drought tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: BIG Data Center PRJCA039106; tissue-expression reference SRA PRJNA665594
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Moderate drought priming; recurrent drought stress; 15% PEG6000 for 24 h
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CsCHS; CsCHI; CsFLS; CsDFR; CsANS; CsANR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transcriptional activator in yeast and tobacco. Identified by phylogeny as closest to AtMYB114.
